title: Select
description: Foldkit-native Base UI Select primitive preserving model-owned open, value, and highlighted-item state, popup/listbox ARIA, keyboard navigation, typeahead, grouping, labels, separators, scroll controls, selected and disabled item data attributes, deterministic placement attributes, and hidden form input wiring.
distinguishes: Foldkit-native Base UI Select primitive preserving model-owned open, value, and highlighted-item state, popup/listbox ARIA, keyboard navigation, typeahead, grouping, labels, separators, scroll controls, selected and disabled item data attributes, deterministic placement attributes, and hidden form input wiring.

registry.reason: Select uses the shared Foldkit lifecycle adapter for origin-equivalent anchored geometry.
Select reuses the local Popover placement and focus command conventions without introducing a competing positioning or dismiss system.

runtime.reason: Provides Html, Attribute, Command, and keyboard event helpers for the Foldkit-native primitive.
Provides Effect Schema, Option, and predicate helpers for serializable options and event payloads.

development.reason: Origin Base UI React Select primitive is replaced by this Foldkit implementation.
Origin Base UI React Select primitive is replaced by this Foldkit implementation.
React is origin fixture evidence only, not installable Foldkit source.

deviations.summary: Open, selected value, and highlighted item are model-owned inputs and interactions emit typed facts instead of mutating internal component state.
deviations.rationale: Foldkit preserves unidirectional data flow and keeps selection state in the consuming model.

deviations.summary: Focus handoff and restoration reuse the Popover command-facing helpers instead of hiding side effects inside render helpers.
deviations.rationale: Select side effects must stay at Foldkit's Command boundary. The installable view remains pure and deterministic.

deviations.summary: Placement is represented through deterministic side, align, offset, collision, positioner, and popup attributes without DOM measurement or collision flipping.
deviations.rationale: Measured collision behavior belongs behind a shared measurement service, not an ad hoc Select render helper side effect.
